title: Quiz: Quantum Proofs
description: Test your understanding of quantum interactive proofs and zero-knowledge

questions.question: What is the surprising result about QIP (quantum interactive proofs)?
questions.explanation: QIP = PSPACE, which means quantum interactive proofs have exactly the same power as classical interactive proofs (IP = PSPACE). Quantum communication in interactive proofs doesn't provide additional computational power.

questions.question: What is MIP* (multi-prover interactive proofs with entanglement) equal to?
questions.explanation: MIP* = RE was proven in 2020 by Ji, Natarajan, Vidick, Wright, and Yuen. This remarkable result shows that entangled provers can verify even undecidable languages, and it also negatively resolved the Connes embedding conjecture.

questions.question: What is the main challenge for quantum zero-knowledge proofs?

options.text: Quantum verifiers can entangle with the proof, complicating simulation
NP problems cannot have quantum proofs
Zero-knowledge is impossible in quantum settings

questions.explanation: A key challenge is that quantum verifiers can entangle with the proof state, making the classical rewinding technique problematic. Watrous developed a quantum rewinding lemma to handle this.

questions.question: Every language in NP has a quantum zero-knowledge proof secure against quantum verifiers.
questions.explanation: True. Watrous (2009) showed that every language in NP has a computational zero-knowledge proof that is secure even against polynomial-time quantum verifiers, using quantum commitment schemes and the quantum rewinding lemma.

questions.question: In the CHSH game, what is the maximum winning probability with entangled provers?
questions.explanation: With entangled provers (sharing a Bell state), the maximum winning probability is cos^2(pi/8) which is about 85.4%, known as the Tsirelson bound. Classical strategies are limited to 75%.

questions.question: How many rounds of communication are sufficient for QIP to equal PSPACE?
questions.explanation: Remarkably, QIP(3) = QIP = PSPACE. Just 3 rounds of quantum communication suffice to achieve the full power of PSPACE. This is a significant result showing quantum communication is very efficient for interactive proofs.

questions.question: What is the classical analog of MIP (without entanglement)?
questions.explanation: Classical MIP = NEXP (non-deterministic exponential time). This is much smaller than MIP* = RE, showing that entanglement between provers dramatically increases the power of multi-prover proofs.

questions.question: Which are applications of quantum proof systems? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Quantum proofs enable quantum money (no-cloning prevents forgery), delegated computation verification (prove server computed correctly), and certified randomness (Bell tests prove quantum origin). They do NOT solve NP-complete problems efficiently.
